food_subgroup: Lean ground beef, raw; 90/10 lean-to-fat ratio; moderate fat (~10g/100g); haem iron source

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for 90/10 lean ground beef raw; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.description: 100g raw lean beef mince — reference standard
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 100g raw portion of lean beef mince (90/10) is the reference serving, equivalent to a small burger patty or the raw weight used in a single-serve bolognese portion before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, Ground, 90% Lean / 10% Fat, Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 174036)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 174036: Beef, Ground, 90% Lean / 10% Fat, Raw. All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from this approved primary source record. No adaptation or estimation required. USDA SR Legacy is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy for raw ingredients and staples.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 174036
gi_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
gi_source.source_match_notes: Lean beef mince (90/10, raw) contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates and produces no glycaemic response. Per Veritas q4 rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable. GL per 100g serving = 0.0. University of Sydney GI Database does not index pure protein/fat foods with zero carbohydrate as GI testing requires a minimum carbohydrate load.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 72mg/100g raw — naturally very low
hypertension.guidance: At 72mg sodium per 100g raw, lean beef mince is a low-sodium protein source suitable for hypertension management; however, the saturated fat content (3.8g/100g) warrants attention given its contribution to arterial stiffness when consumed in excess, and cooking methods that add sodium (e.g., seasoned sauces) should be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate; high protein supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0g carbohydrate and a GI that is not applicable, lean beef mince does not raise blood glucose; its high protein content (20.1g/100g) promotes satiety and supports insulin-independent glucose regulation, making it a suitable protein choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when prepared without high-sugar or high-starch sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of 3.8g per 100g raw (approximately 5.6g per 100g cooked after moisture loss) contributes meaningfully to daily saturated fat intake; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit total red meat consumption to no more than 500g cooked per week per WHO guidance, choose 90/10 over 80/20 mince to reduce saturated fat by approximately 1.9g per 100g, and drain rendered fat after browning to further reduce intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat saturated fat and pro-inflammatory potential at high intake volumes
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Red meat consumption above 500g cooked per week is associated with increased inflammatory markers in observational RA studies; lean 90/10 mince reduces saturated fat exposure compared to regular mince, and its haem iron (2.1mg/100g, high bioavailability) and zinc (4.5mg/100g) support immune enzyme function when consumed in moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lean Beef Mince (90/10, Raw) is raw, uncooked beef that has been finely ground to a 90% lean / 10% fat ratio, displaying a deep red colour with a soft, malleable texture. It is sold fresh or frozen at major Singapore supermarkets including NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and Don Don Donki. Per 100g raw: 176 kcal, 20.1g protein, 10.1g total fat (3.8g saturated, 4.3g monounsaturated, 0.4g polyunsaturated, 0.1g trans), 0g carbohydrate, 72mg sodium, 69.6g water. Key micronutrients: iron 2.1mg (haem iron, approximately 12% RDA — high bioavailability), zinc 4.5mg (approximately 41% RDA), vitamin B12 2.1mcg (approximately 88% RDA), phosphorus 198mg, potassium 319mg, selenium 16.1mcg. Glycemic index is not applicable — zero carbohydrate content means no glycaemic response occurs. Cooking yield: 100g raw yields approximately 68g cooked (32% moisture and fat loss during browning). Draining rendered fat after browning saves an additional 2–3g fat per 100g raw. Choosing 90/10 over 80/20 mince saves approximately 78 kcal and 1.9g saturated fat per 100g. Suitable for bolognese, burger patties, stir-fries, meatballs, and stuffed vegetables. Clinically, it is a suitable protein source for Type 2 Diabetes (zero carbohydrate, high satiety protein) and hypertension (low sodium at 72mg/100g), but cardiovascular disease patients should monitor total red meat intake and saturated fat contribution. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036. Confidence: high.
common_user_queries: how much protein in beef mince
is beef mince good for weight loss

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values are sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 174036 (Beef, Ground, 90% Lean / 10% Fat, Raw), an exact match to the food described. No estimation or adaptation was required. USDA SR Legacy is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy for raw ingredients. Full nutrient panel coverage including fatty acids, vitamins, minerals, and water content.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is animal muscle tissue containing no digestible carbohydrate — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036. Vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036. Water_g set to 69.6g per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036 (moisture content reported as 69.57g/100g, rounded to 69.6g). Monounsaturated_fat_g (4.3g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4g) are sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036 fatty acid breakdown. Glycemic index is not applicable — lean beef mince (90/10, raw) contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g per USDA SR Legacy FDC 174036; no glycaemic response occurs. GI set to null and gi_category set to not_applicable per Veritas q4 rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods. University of Sydney GI Database does not index pure protein/fat foods with zero carbohydrate as GI testing requires a minimum carbohydrate load; the USDA source confirming zero carbohydrate is the basis for this determination. This record is a generic raw ingredient reference; it does not represent any specific branded product. Halal status reflects the general category; consumers should verify halal certification of specific retail products. Cooking yield reference: 100g raw yields approximately 68g cooked per USDA cooking yield data for ground beef. FoodEx2 code A00HA and FoodOn URI FOODON_03315023 are carried forward from the input record and are defensible direct matches. SNOMED CT code is null — no specific SNOMED CT concept for 90/10 lean ground beef raw was identified; this is documented as a gap.
